<!--
Thanks for contributing to Argentor! Please fill out the sections below.
Keep PRs small and focused — one logical change per PR.
-->

## Description

<!-- What does this PR do? Why is it needed? Link to design notes or discussion if applicable. -->

## Type of change

<!-- Check all that apply -->

- [ ] Bug fix (non-breaking change that fixes an issue)
- [ ] New feature (non-breaking change that adds functionality)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that changes existing behavior)
- [ ] Documentation update
- [ ] Refactor / internal cleanup (no behavior change)
- [ ] Performance improvement
- [ ] Test-only change
- [ ] CI / tooling change

## Related issue

<!-- If this closes an issue, reference it here. Remove the line if not applicable. -->

Closes #

## How has this been tested?

<!-- Describe the tests you ran, new tests you added, and how reviewers can verify the change. -->

## Testing checklist

- [ ] Added or updated unit tests for the change
- [ ] `cargo test --workspace` passes locally
- [ ] `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` passes
- [ ] `cargo fmt --all -- --check` passes
- [ ] Manually verified the change end-to-end (describe how above)

## Documentation checklist

- [ ] Rustdoc added or updated on public items touched by this PR
- [ ] User-facing docs updated (`docs/`, `README.md`) when relevant
- [ ] `CHANGELOG.md` updated with a user-visible entry (Added / Changed / Fixed / Removed)
- [ ] Public API changes are noted as such (breaking or additive)

## Security considerations

<!-- Does this PR touch capabilities, sandboxing, input validation, crypto, or secrets handling? -->

- [ ] No security impact
- [ ] Reviewed for input validation, capability leaks, and sandbox escape
- [ ] New dependencies vetted (license, maintenance, security advisories)

## Additional notes for reviewers

<!-- Areas you would like extra eyes on, known trade-offs, follow-up work, etc. -->

---

<!--
Commit conventions:
- Use Conventional Commits (feat:, fix:, docs:, refactor:, test:, chore:, perf:, ci:)
- Do NOT add "Co-Authored-By" or AI attribution lines — per project policy
-->
